content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 607 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance · · Administration · · Enforcement · · Executive/Legal · · Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5660        322-5660           322-5951          322-6441              322-6444

April 1, 1981

Phillip O. Ormsbee
District Secretary
Bay Area Rapid Transit District
600 Madison Street
Oakland, CA 94607

                                                    A-81-04-041

Re: Response to your letter
    of December 10, 1980

Dear Mr. Ormsbee:

    Pursuant to the request of Carol Asker of your office
we have reviewed our files for a copy of our response to
your letter of December 10, 1980. We are unable to locate a
copy of such response. I will respond herewith to your
question.

    Our regulation, 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18752(b), (c)
and (d) provides that:

    (b) A state or a local government agency with
jurisdiction in more than one county may make nonsubstantive alterations of a conflict of interest code for
its agency. Nonsubstantive alterations shall become
effective on an interim basis upon approval as provided
in paragraph (c) of this section and shall be submitted
to the Fair Political Practices Commission for permanent
approval by January 31 of the year succeeding the calendar
year in which the nonsubstantive alteration is made.

    (c) No alteration of a conflict of interest code
shall be deemed nonsubstantive until the agency shall
have requested and received prior written approval from
the Executive Director of the Fair Political Practices
Commission, or his or her designee, to classify the
alteration as nonsubstantive. Each request for a nonsubstantive alteration shall be in writing setting
forth the proposed language change and a brief explanation. The Executive Director, or his or her designee,

Phillip O. Ormsbee
April 1, 1981
Page Two


shall respond in writing to each request for interim
approval within 30 calendar days of receipt.

(d) Nonsubstantive alterations of conflict of
interest codes shall be limited to the following:

(1) The reclassification or renaming of
previously designated positions, provided no desig-
nated positions are created or deleted, and provided
no existing disclosure responsibilities are modified;

. . .

.(Emphasis added.)

In addition, this section requires that permanent adoption
of such alterations by your agency must comply with Government
Code Section 87311. You are permitted to "save-up" your
interim alterations and do them once a year as permanent
amendments to receive interim approval. In October, you
will receive notice from us reminding of the requirement for
permanent adoption through the normal procedures under the
Administrative Procedure Act.

If you have further questions, please contact me.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Robert E. Leigh
Counsel
Legal Division

REL:plh

sections.question_synthetic: What procedures apply to altering a conflict of interest code for a multi-county government agency, and what types of alterations are considered nonsubstantive?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Nonsubstantive alterations to conflict of interest codes must receive prior written approval from the FPPC Executive Director within 30 days. Only reclassification or renaming of positions without creating new positions, deleting existing ones, or modifying disclosure responsibilities qualifies as nonsubstantive. Permanent adoption must comply with Government Code Section 87311.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No discrete QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; letter is structured as continuous prose response to prior inquiry. The substantive legal guidance is embedded in regulatory citation and explanation rather than a formal conclusion section.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What procedures apply to altering a conflict of interest code for a multi-county government agency, and what types of alterations are considered nonsubstantive?

CONCLUSION: Nonsubstantive alterations to conflict of interest codes must receive prior written approval from the FPPC Executive Director within 30 days. Only reclassification or renaming of positions without creating new positions, deleting existing ones, or modifying disclosure responsibilities qualifies as nonsubstantive. Permanent adoption must comply with Government Code Section 87311.
embedding.first_500_words: April 1, 1981 Phillip O. Ormsbee District Secretary Bay Area Rapid Transit District 800 Madison Street Oakland, CA 94607 Re: Response to your letter of December 10, 1980 Dear Mr. Ormsbee: Pursuant to the request of Carol Asker of your office we have reviewed our files for a copy of our response to your letter of December 10, 1980. We are unable to locate a copy of such response. I will respond herewith to your question. Our regulation, 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18752(b), (c) and (d) provides that: (b) A state or a local government agency with jurisdiction in more than one county may make nonsubstantive alterations of a conflict of interest code for its agency. Nonsubstantive alterations shall become effective on an interim basis upon approval as provided in paragraph (c) of this section and shall be submitted to the Fair Political Practices Commission for permanent approval by January 31 of the year succeeding the calendar year in which the nonsubstantive alteration is made. (c) No alteration of a conflict of interest code shall be deemed nonsubstantive until the agency shall have requested and received prior written approval from the Executive Director of the Fair Political Practices Commission, or his or her designee, to classify the alteration as nonsubstantive. Each request for a nonsubstantive alteration shall be in writing setting forth the proposed language change and a brief explanation. The Executive Director, or his or her designee, shall respond in writing to each request for interim approval within 30 calendar days of receipt. (d) Nonsubstantive alterations of conflict of interest codes shall be limited to the following: (1) The reclassification or renaming of previously designated positions, provided no designated positions are created or deleted, and provided no existing disclosure responsibilities are modified; .(Emphasis added.) In addition, this section requires that permanent adoption of such alterations by your agency must comply with Government Code Section 87311. You are permitted to "save-up" your interim alterations and do them once a year as permanent amendments to receive interim approval. In October, you will receive notice from us reminding of the requirement for permanent adoption through the normal procedures under the Administrative Procedure Act. If you have further questions, please contact me. Sincerely, Robert E. Leidigh Counsel Legal Division REL:plh
embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to the Bay Area Rapid Transit District regarding procedures for making nonsubstantive alterations to its conflict of interest code, citing relevant regulations and approval requirements.
